rules.description: Owned objects crossing a public ABI use opaque handles instead of exposing implementation structs.
rules.proof: public header validator and consumer tests

rules.description: ABI-visible option/config/descriptor structs include struct_size and callers initialize it to sizeof(struct_type).
Public ABI config structs include an API version when forward/backward compatibility is required.
Cross-boundary allocation uses an explicit allocator or a matching destroy/free API.
Public APIs document ownership, lifetime, threading, and destroy responsibilities.
Public APIs return explicit result or refusal codes instead of silent failure.
Public C ABI does not expose C++ classes, templates, STL types, exceptions, or overload-only symbols.
Engine public headers do not include Windows, POSIX, Cocoa, OpenGL, Direct3D, Vulkan, Metal, SDL, or equivalent platform headers.
Public memory layout is not a file, network, save, or replay encoding.
Public callback APIs carry a void *user pointer and explicit lifetime/threading rules.
Public reusable symbols use domino_ and public Dominium product symbols use dominium_.
Public headers are guarded, self-contained where practical, and avoid private implementation includes.
rules.proof: public header validator and consumer compile tests

rules.description: Stable C public headers compile as C89 unless the surface declares a higher language floor.
